mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444491M as of 2014-10-01 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) declining below 5.5% signals labor market tightening and wage pressure, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.8% as of 2014-10-01, approaching threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market normalization
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 5.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, creating negative real rates that devalue cash and drive equity valuations higher, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 237.633 as of 2014-10-01
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary accommodation with emerging inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.5% month-over-month, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability expansion, driving negative 5-day forward returns as the market front-runs the earnings surprise and reprices risk upward.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with supply constraints emerging
failure_condition: When INDPRO MoM > 1.5%, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC is negative and statistically insignificant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors. This joint signal produces positive 20-day forward returns as equity markets price in sustained economic momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1017K and RSAFS at 444491M as of 2014-09-01 and 2014-10-01
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 62% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K per month, signaling labor market weakness and triggering expectations of Federal Reserve rate cuts, which compress equity discount rates and produce positive 60-day forward returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with monetary policy pivot
failure_condition: When PAYEMS 3-month growth < 100K, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 56% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (proxied by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) turn negative and remain below -1.5%, creating a financial repression regime that forces equity allocation and drives positive 20-day forward returns as portfolio rebalancing accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:Near-zero FEDFUNDS with moderate inflation creates negative real rates
regime_hint: financial repression with zero lower bound monetary policy
failure_condition: When real rates < -1.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 61% of instances, or the predictive power decays to Rank IC < 0.02 after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized, signaling robust economic expansion and earnings growth. This always-on trend signal produces positive 60-day forward returns as the market reprices growth expectations upward without requiring threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth fails to produce positive forward returns in > 58% of instances.
